// ABOUT THIS FILE: Master reference for auto-event templates. Files starting with '_' are NEVER loaded as templates — this one exists purely as documentation. Copy a real example (ae00001) to make a new template; every option below is explained by the '//key' line above it.
// HOW IT WORKS: The daily midnight cron checks every enabled template; if its cadence/spawn day is due and it has no live event, it generates one, verifies every round is beatable, creates a normal event (played via cd-pe), and announces it. Admins: cd-ae list / cd-ae spawn <id>.
//name: Base event name. Spawns are named '<name> <roman numeral>[: <subtitle>]' — e.g. 'Proving Grounds XIV: Trial by Fire'. The numeral comes from this template's lifetime spawn counter.
name: Master Example
//enabled: Master switch. false = the cron and cd-ae spawn both refuse it. Ship new templates disabled, test with cd-ae spawn after enabling.
//generator: Which generator builds the roster. Registered generators live in autoEvents.js — currently only 'provinggrounds' (solvability-verified gauntlet). Future generators plug in there.
//cadenceDays: Spawn every N days (measured from the last spawn, checked daily at midnight). Ignored if spawnDay is set. A template never spawns while its previous event is still active.
//spawnDay: Alternative to cadenceDays: spawn on this weekday (e.g. 'wednesday'). null = use cadenceDays. Good for themed weeklies like a BM-only Wednesday.
//durationDays: How long the spawned event stays active (its deadline). Shorter than the cadence = a gap between runs (scarcity); equal = back-to-back.
//rounds: Rounds in the gauntlet, 1-30 (event system limit). Difficulty interpolates across them.
//difficulty: Difficulty curve from first to last round, each 1-10. 1 ≈ 12% of the theme universe can win; 10 ≈ a handful. If end ≥ 9, the FINAL round is forced to an absolute 1-5 solver cars ('do you own THE answer'). A gentler event might use {start: 1, end: 6}.
//entryFee: One-time money fee charged at a player's first race of the event (0 = free). Shown in the race confirm before charging.
//uniqueSolutionThreshold: Rounds whose solver count is at or below this get pairwise-DISJOINT solutions — no single car can clear two of them, so deep runs require genuinely distinct answers. 0 disables.
//subtitles: Random subtitle pool for unthemed spawns (themed spawns use the theme's own name). Empty array = no subtitle.
//theme: null/'none' = never themed. 'auto' = roll from themeRecipes each spawn. Or a FIXED object: { "name": "Black Market Mayhem", "baseReqs": { "cardType": ["abm", "ibm", "pbm"] }, "universe": "bm" } — baseReqs are merged into every round; 'universe': 'bm' switches the solvability universe to BM cards (only for deliberate BM-only events; omit otherwise).
//themeRecipes: Used when theme = 'auto'. Weighted roll per spawn; 'none' weight keeps most spawns unthemed. Each recipe samples a concrete value from real car data and validates at least minCars (default 100) match before committing. Kinds: rarity (CR bracket, 'Legendary Gauntlet'), make ('Mazda Showcase'), country ('Made in JP'), decade ('1980s Throwback'), cardType (player-side restriction, e.g. normal-only 'No Crutches' — values is a list of cardType arrays to choose from).
//themeOpponents: true (default) = property-themed spawns also draw OPPONENTS from the theme when viable (fight legendaries WITH legendaries). cardType/BM themes always use the open opponent pool.
//rewards: Everything below. All rewards are delivered through cd-rewards with the event's name as origin, once per round per player (first clear).

rewards.//moneyBudget: HARD CAP on total money this event can pay one player. 0 = a pure no-money event (any money in milestones then fails the spawn — purity guard). If milestones use "$BUDGET" money, the remaining budget is split across those slots by moneyCurve.
rewards.//moneyCurve: How "$BUDGET" money spreads across its slots: 'flat' (even), 'quadratic' (later rounds pay more), 'finale' (everything on the last budget slot).
rewards.//carPool: Cars the "$CARPOOL" sentinel draws from (avoids repeating the previous spawn's pick; distinct within one event). Entries must exist; non-Prize entries log a warning. The drawn car is featured in the spawn announcement.
rewards.//milestones: roundNumber → reward object. Keys: money (number or "$BUDGET"), trophies, fuseTokens, car (carID or "$CARPOOL"), pack (packID or "$PACKPOOL"). Rounds without an entry pay nothing — the climb itself is the content.
